Project  Description  
 
This  project  focuses  on  designing  a  database  system  to  manage  fleets  of  
humanoid
 
robots
 
used
 
in
 
environments
 
such
 
as
 
warehouses,
 
hospitals,
 
and
 
homes.
 
As
 
humanoid
 
robots
 
become
 
more
 
common,
 
they
 
generate
 
a
 
large
 
amount
 
of
 
information,
 
including
 
task
 
assignments,
 
task
 
execution
 
history,
 
deployment
 
locations,
 
energy
 
levels,
 
maintenance
 
records,
 
and
 
interaction
 
details.
 
Currently,
 
much
 
of
 
this
 
information
 
is
 
either
 
spread
 
across
 
different
 
tools
 
or
 
not
 
stored
 
in
 
a
 
structured
 
way,
 
making
 
it
 
difficult
 
to
 
track
 
robot
 
behavior
 
and
 
performance
 
over
 
time.
 
There  are  existing  robot  fleet  management  platforms,  such  as  Formant  and  
InOrbit
,
 
that
 
help
 
monitor
 
robots
 
and
 
show
 
their
 
current
 
status.
 
These
 
systems
 
are
 
useful
 
for
 
real-time
 
monitoring
 
and
 
coordination,
 
but
 
they
 
are
 
mainly
 
designed
 
for
 
general
 
robot
 
fleets
 
and
 
do
 
not
 
focus
 
on
 
organizing
 
long-term
 
data
 
for
 
humanoid
 
robots.
 
In
 
particular,
 
they
 
do
 
not
 
emphasize
 
storing
 
detailed
 
interaction
 
context,
 
coordination
 
roles,
 
or
 
historical
 
data
 
in
 
a
 
centralized
 
database.
 
One  unique  feature  of  the  system  is  its  ability  to  store  human  emotional  
context
 
data
 
in
 
personal
 
and
 
healthcare
 
environments.
 
In
 
settings
 
such
 
as
 
hospitals
 
or
 
homes,
 
humanoid
 
robots
 
often
 
interact
 
closely
 
with
 
people.
 
The
 
system
 
allows
 
simple
 
emotional
 
observations,
 
such
 
as
 
calm
 
or
 
urgent
 
situations,
 
to
 
be
 
recorded
 
as
 
data
 
for
 
later
 
analysis.
 
Most
 
existing
 
fleet
 
management
 
tools
 
do
 
not
 
capture
 
or
 
organize
 
this
 
type
 
of
 
information.
 
Another  unique  feature  is  role-based  robot  coordination  in  warehouse  
environments.
 
The
 
system
 
allows
 
certain
 
robots
 
to
 
be
 
assigned
 
a
 
senior
 
role
 
to
 
help
 
coordinate
 
tasks
 
among
 
other
 
robots
 
based
 
on
 
factors
 
such
 
as
 
hardware
 
capability
 
and
 
energy
 
level.
 
If
 
a
 
senior
 
robot
 
becomes
 
unavailable,
 
the
 
system
 
supports
 
reassigning
 
that
 
role
 
to
 
another
 
robot,
 
helping
 
prevent
 
disruptions.
 
Existing
 
tools
 
typically
 
treat
 
all
 
robots
 
equally
 
and
 
do
 
not
 
support
 
this
 
type
 
of
 
structured
 
coordination
 
with
 
a
 
fail-safe
 
mechanism.
 
Overall,  this  project  provides  a  centralized  and  structured  way  to  manage  
humanoid
 
robot
 
data
 
that
 
existing
 
solutions
 
do
 
not
 
fully
 
address.
 
Manufacturers,
 
warehouses,
 
hospitals,
 
and
 
households
 
deploying
 
humanoid
 
robots
 
can
 
benefit
 
from
 
a
 
system
 
that
 
keeps
 
robot
 
information
 
organized,
 
reliable,
 
and
 
easy
 
to
 
understand
 
over
 
time.

Functional  Database  Requirements  
  
1.  Robot  (Strong)  1.1.  A  robot  shall  belong  at  most  one  robot  model.  1.2.  A  robot  shall  be  deployed  at  at  most  one  deployment  location  at  a  time.  1.3.  A  robot  shall  be  assigned  at  most  one  role  at  a  time.  1.4.  A  robot  shall  have  many   task  assignments.  1.5.  A  robot  shall  participate  in  many  task  executions.  1.6.  A  robot  shall  have  many  emotion  records  over  time.  1.7.  A  robot  shall  have  at  most  one  user.  1.8.  A  robot  shall  have  many  energy  status  records  over  time.  1.9.  A  robot  shall  generate  many  logs.  1.10.  A  robot  may  be  associated  with  many  RobotHardware.  1.11.  A  robot  shall  have  at  most  one  software.  1.12.  A  robot  shall  have  many  performance  metrics.  1.13.  A  robot  shall  generate  many  alerts.  1.14.  A  robot  shall  have  many  diagnostic  reports  over  time.  1.15.  A  robot  shall  have  many  sensors  1.16.  A  robot  shall  charge  at  many  stations  1.17.  A  robot  shall  have  many  charging  sessions  1.18.  A  robot  shall  have  a  current  status   
2.  Robot  Model  (Strong)  2.1.  A  robot  model  shall  support  zero  or  many  hardware  components.  2.2.  A  robot  model  shall  be  produced  by  at  most  one  manufacturer.   2.3.  A  robot  model  shall  be  associated  with  many  robots.   
3.  Operator  (User)  (Strong)  3.1.  An  operator  shall  interact  with  many  robots.  3.2.  An  operator  may  participate  in  many  task  assignments.  3.3.  An  operator  may  generate  many  support  requests.  3.4.  An  operator  may  participate  in  many  interaction  sessions.  3.5.  An  operator  shall  be  assigned  many  deployment  locations.

4.  Manufacturer  (Strong)  4.1.  A  manufacturer  shall  produce  many  robot  models.  4.2.  A  manufacturer  shall  produce  many  hardware.  4.3.  A  manufacturer  shall  provide  zero  or  many  software  versions.  
 
 
5.  Hardware(Strong)  5.1.  A  hardware  component  shall  be  produced  by  exactly  one  manufacturer.  5.2.  A  hardware  component  shall  participate  in   many  robot  hardware  records.(loop)  5.3.  A  hardware  component  shall  be  supported  by   many  robot  models.(loop)    
6.  Robot  Hardware(Weak)  6.1.  A  robot  hardware  record  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  6.2.  A  robot  hardware  record  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  hardware  component.  
  
7.  Software  Version  (Strong)  7.1.  A  software  version  shall  be  associated  with  many  robots  7.2.  A  software  version  shall  be  provided  by  exactly  one  manufacturer.   
8.  Deployment  Location   (Strong-Superclass)  8.1.  A  deployment  location  shall  host  many  robots.  8.2.  A  deployment  location  shall  support  different  environment  types  
(warehouse,
 
hospital,
 
household).
 8.3.  A  deployment  location  shall  belong  to  at  most   one  address.  8.4.  A  deployment  location  shall  host  many  users  8.5.  A  deployment  location  shall  have  many  logs  8.6.  A  deployment  location  shall  have  many  charging  sessions   9.  Address  (Strong)  9.1.  An  address  may  be  associated  with  many  deployment  locations.  9.2.  An  address  shall  store  street,  city,  state,  and  zipcode  information.

10.  Task  (Strong) 10.1.  A  task  shall  be  associated  with   many  task  assignments.  10.2.  A  task  shall  be  associated  with  many  task  executions.   
11.  Task  Assignment  (weak)  11.1.  A  task  assignment  shall  associate  exactly  one  robot  .  11.2.  A  task  assignment  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  operator.  11.3.  A  task  assignment  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  task.   
12.  Task  Execution  (Weak)  12.1.  A  task  execution  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  12.2.  A  task  execution  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  task.  12.3.  A  task  execution  shall  generate  at  least  one  log    
13.  Emotion  Record  (Weak) 13.1.  An  emotion  record  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  interaction  session.  13.2.  An  emotion  record  shall  store  observed  emotional  state  and  timestamp.  
 
14.  InteractionSession(Weak)  14.1.  An  interaction  session  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  14.2.  An  interaction  session  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  operator.   15.  Robot  Role  (Strong)  15.1.  A  robot  role  shall  be  assigned  to  many  robots.  15.2.  A  robot  role  may  be  recommended  by  AI  responses.   
16.  Energy  Status  (Weak)  16.1.  An  energy  status  shall  be  associated  with  exactly  one  robot.  16.2.  An  energy  status  shall   be  linked  to  one  charging  session.   17.  PerformanceMetric(Weak)  17.1.  A  performance  metrics   shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  17.2.  A  performance  shall  have  a  time  stamp  record.

18.  Alert(Weak)  18.1.  An  alert  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  18.2.  An  alert  shall  record  severity  and  time.  18.3.  An  alert  may  be  associated  with  one  AI  request.   19.  DiagnosticReport(Weak)  19.1.  A  diagnostic  report  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  19.2.  A  diagnostic  report  shall  have  performance  metrics.  19.3.  A  diagnostic  report  shall  be  associated  with  one  AI  model.    
20.  Log   (weak)  20.1.  A  log  shall  be  associated  with  one  robot.  20.2.  A  log  shall  record  operational  events.  20.3.  A  log  shall  be  associated  with  at  most  one  deployment  location   
21.  Sensor  (Weak)  21.1.  A  sensor  shall  be  associated  with  one  robot  21.2.  A  sensor  shall  generate  at  least  one  log   
22.  Charging  Station  (Strong)  22.1.  A  charging  station  shall  serve  many  robots  22.2.  A  charging  station  shall  record  many  charging  sessions   
23.  Charging  Session  (Weak)  23.1.  A  charging  session  shall  occur  at  one  charging  station  23.2.  A  charging  session  shall  be  associated  with  one  robot  23.3.  A  charging  session  shall  be  associated  with  one  deployment  location  23.4.  A  charging  session  shall  generate  at  least  one  log   
24.  Support  Request  (Weak)  24.1.  A  support  request  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  24.2.  A  support  request  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  operator.  24.3.  A  support  request  shall  record  issue  details  and  time.   
25.  AI  Model  (Strong) 25.1.  An  AI  model  shall  generate  many  AI  requests.

25.2.  An  AI  model  shall  analyze  zero  or  many  diagnostic  reports.  25.3.  An  AI  model  shall  store  service  type.   
26.  AI  Request(Weak) 26.1.  An  AI  request  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  AI  model.  26.2.  An  AI  request  shall  reference  one  robot.  26.3.  An  AI  request  shall  analyze   at  least  one   emotion  record.    
27.  AI  Response  (Weak)  27.1.  An  AI  response  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  AI  request.  27.2.  An  AI  response  shall  recommend  exactly  one  robot  role.  27.3.  An  AI  response  shall  recommend  one  task.    
28.  Hospital  (Strong-Subclass) 28.1.  A  hospital  shall  belong  to  at  most  one  deployment  location  28.2.  A  hospital  shall  have  many  departments.  28.3.  A  hospital  shall  have  many  emotion  records.   
29.  Warehouse  (Strong-Subclass) 29.1.  A  warehouse  shall  belong  to  at  most  one  deployment  location  29.2.  A  warehouse  shall  host  many  robots.  29.3.  A  warehouse  shall   have  many  storage  zones.    
30.  Home  (Strong-Subclass) 30.1.  A  home  shall  belong  to  at  most  one  deployment  location  30.2.  A  home  may  host  many  robots.  30.3.  A  home  may  record  many  interaction  sessions.   
31.  Factory  (Strong-Subclass)  31.1.  A  factory  shall  belong  to  at  most  one  deployment  location  31.2.  A  factory  shall  have  many  robots

31.3.  A     
32.  Department(Weak) 32.1.  A  department  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  hospital.  32.2.  A  department  may  host  many  robots.  32.3.  A  department  may  record  many  interaction  sessions.  
 
33.   EmotionalPattern  (Weak)  33.1.  An  emotional  pattern  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  33.2.  An  emotional  pattern  shall  summarize  at  least  one   emotion  record.  33.3.  An  emotional  pattern  shall  be  analyzed  by  exactly  one  AI  model.  
 
34.  AccessPermission  (Strong)   34.1.  An  access  permission  shall  belong  to  at  most  one  robot  role.  34.2.  An  access  permission  shall  define  access  level.  34.3.  An  access  permission  shall  apply  to   many  operators.  
 
35.  UserRole  (Strong) 35.1.  A  user  role  shall  classify   many  operators.  35.2.  A  user  role  shall  be  associated  with   many  access  permissions.  35.3.  A  user  role  shall  define  authority  level.  
  
36.  MaintenanceRecord  (Weak)  36.1.  A  maintenance  record  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  36.2.  A  maintenance  record  shall  reference  zero  or  many  hardware  
components.
 
 36.3.  A  maintenance  record  shall  be  performed  by  exactly  one  technician.

Non-Functional  Database  Requirements  
 
1.  Performance  1.1.  The  system  shall  support  concurrent  access  by  at  least  _  robots  without  
performance
 
degradation.
 1.2.  The  system  shall  be  able  to  handle  concurrent  usage  by  _  robots  1.3.  The  system  shall  be  able  to  maintain  performance  while  receiving  AI  
driven
 
queries
 
for
 
real
 
time
 
data
 
 
2.  Security  2.1.  Any  logs  and  data  shall  only  be  able  to  be  accessed  by  an  user  with  
sufficient
 
permissions
 2.2.  The  system  shall  protect  AI  request  and  AI  response  data  from  
unauthorized
 
access.
  
3.  Reliability  3.1.  The  system  shall  retain  operational  logs  for  at  least  12  months.  3.2.  The  system  shall  have  daily  backups

4.  Maintainability  4.1.   The  system  shall  host  AI  queries  and  structural  queries  separately  4.2.  The  database  schema  shall  be  well  documented.  
 5.  Scalability  5.1.  The  system  shall  support  growth  in  the  number  of  robots  and  deployment  
locations.
 5.2.  The  system  shall  support  increasing  volumes  of  task  execution  and  logs  
data.
 5.3.   The  system  shall  support  any  future  AI  integration.    
 6.  Privacy  6.1.  Emotional  interaction  records  shall  be  stored  securely  to  protect  human-  
related
 
sensitive
 
data.
 6.2.  Access  to  emotional  data  shall  be  restricted  to  authorized  persons  only  . .

11.  Task  Assignment  (weak)  11.1.  A  task  assignment  shall  associate  exactly  one  robot  .  11.2.  A  task  assignment  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  operator.  11.3.  A  task  assignment  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  task.   
12.  Task  Execution  (Weak)  12.1.  A  task  execution  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  12.2.  A  task  execution  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  task.  12.3.  A  task  execution  shall  generate  at  least  one  log   
13.  Emotion  Record  (Weak)  13.1.  An  emotion  record  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  interaction  session.  13.2.  An  emotion  record  shall  belong  to  at  least  one  hospital.  13.3.  An  emotion  record  shall  have  at  least  one  Ai  request.  13.4.  An  emotion  record  shall  be  summarized  by  one  emotional  pattern   
14.  InteractionSession(Weak)  14.1.  An  interaction  session  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  14.2.  An  interaction  session  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  operator.  14.3.  An  interaction  session  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  home.  
 15.  Robot  Role  (Strong)  15.1.  A  robot  role  shall  be  assigned  to  many  robots.  15.2.  A  robot  role  shall  be  assigned  by  at  most  one  user.  15.3.  A  robot  role  may  be  recommended  by  at  most  one   AI  response.   
16.  Energy  Status  (Weak)  16.1.  An  energy  status  shall  be  associated  with  exactly  one  robot.  16.2.  An  energy  status  shall   be  linked  to  one  charging  session.  16.3.  An  energy  status  shall  have  at  least  one  logs.

17.  PerformanceMetric(Weak)  17.1.  A  performance  metrics   shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  17.2.  A  performance  shall  have  a  time  stamp  record.  17.3.  A  performance  metric  shall  be  associated  with  at  least  one  diagnostic  
report.
  18.  Alert(Weak)  18.1.  An  alert  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  18.2.  An  alert  shall  record  severity  and  time.  18.3.  An  alert  may  be  associated  with  one  AI  request.   19.  DiagnosticReport(Weak)  19.1.  A  diagnostic  report  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  19.2.  A  diagnostic  report  shall  have  performance  metrics.  19.3.  A  diagnostic  report  shall  be  associated  with  one  AI  model.    
20.  Log   (weak)  20.1.  A  log  shall  be  associated  with  one  robot.  20.2.  A  log  shall  be  generated  by  at  least  one  sensor.  20.3.  A  log  shall  have  at  least  one  charging  session  information.   
21.  Sensor  (Weak)  21.1.  A  sensor  shall  be  associated  with  one  robot.  21.2.  A  sensor  shall  generate  at  least  one  log.  21.3.  A  sensor  shall  belong  to  at  least  one  manufacturer.   
22.  Charging  Station  (Strong)  22.1.  A  charging  station  shall  serve  many  robots.  22.2.  A  charging  station  shall  record  many  charging  sessions.  22.3.  A  charging  station  shall  have  many  maintenance  records.   
23.  Charging  Session  (Weak)  23.1.  A  charging  session  shall  occur  at  one  charging  station.  23.2.  A  charging  session  shall  be  associated  with  one  robot.  23.3.  A  charging  session  shall  be  associated  with  one  deployment  location.  23.4.  A  charging  session  shall  generate  at  least  one  log.

24.  Support  Request  (Weak)  24.1.  A  support  request  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  24.2.  A  support  request  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  technician.  24.3.  A  support  request  shall  be  associated  with  at  least  one  maintenance  
record.
 
 
25.  AI  Model  (Strong)  25.1.  An  AI  model  shall  generate  many  AI  requests.  25.2.  An  AI  model  shall  analyze  zero  or  many  diagnostic  reports.  25.3.  An  AI  model  shall  analyze  many  emotional  patterns.   
26.  AI  Request(Weak) 26.1.  An  AI  request  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  AI  model.  26.2.  An  AI  request  shall  reference  one  robot.  26.3.  An  AI  request  shall  have  at  least  one  emotion  record.  26.4.  An  AI  request  shall  belong  to  one  AI  response.   
27.  AI  Response  (Weak)  27.1.  An  AI  response  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  AI  request.  27.2.  An  AI  response  shall  recommend  exactly  one  robot  role.  27.3.  An  AI  response  shall  recommend  one  task.   
28.  Hospital  (Weak-Subclass)  28.1.  A  hospital  shall  belong  to  at  least  one  deployment  location.  28.2.  A  hospital  shall  have  at  least  one  department.  28.3.  A  hospital  shall  have  at  least  one   emotion  record.   
29.  Warehouse  (Weak-Subclass)  29.1.  A  warehouse  shall  belong  to  at  least   one  deployment  location.  29.2.  A  warehouse  shall  host  at  least  one  robot.  29.3.  A  warehouse  shall  have  at  least  one  storage  zone.

30.  Home  (Weak-Subclass)  30.1.  A  home  shall  belong  to  at  least  one  deployment  location.  30.2.  A  home  may  host  at  least  one  robot.  30.3.  A  home  shall  have  at  least  one  interaction  session.   
31.  Factory  (Weak-Subclass) 31.1.  A  factory  shall  belong  to  at  most  one  deployment  location.  31.2.  A  factory  shall  have  at  least  one  robot.  31.3.  A  factory  shall  have  at  least  one   product.   
32.  Department(Weak)
 32.1.  A  department  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  hospital.  32.2.  A  department  may  host  at  least  one  robot.  32.3.  A  department  may  record  at  least  one  interaction  session.  
 
33.   EmotionalPattern  (Weak)  33.1.  An  emotional  pattern  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  33.2.  An  emotional  pattern  shall  summarize  at  least  one   emotion  record.  33.3.  An  emotional  pattern  shall  be  analyzed  by  exactly  one  AI  model.  
  
34.  MaintenanceRecord  (Weak)  34.1.  A  maintenance  record  shall  belong  to  exactly  one  robot.  34.2.  A  maintenance  record  shall  reference  at  least  one  hardware  component.  34.3.  A  maintenance  record  shall  be  performed  by  exactly  one  technician.  
 
35.  Technician  35.1.  A  technician  shall  perform  zero  or  many  maintenance  records.  35.2.  A  technician  shall  resolve  zero  or  many  support  requests.  35.3.  A  technician  shall  be  assigned  to  at  most   one  deployment  location.

Non-Functional  Database  Requirements  
 
1.  Performance  1.1.  The  system  shall  support  concurrent  access  by  at  least  1000  robots  
without
 
performance
 
degradation.
 1.2.  The  system  shall  be  able  to  handle  concurrent  usage  by  100  robots  1.3.  The  system  shall  be  able  to  maintain  performance  while  receiving  AI  
driven
 
queries
 
for
 
real
 
time
 
data
 
 
2.  Security  2.1.  Any  logs  and  data  shall  only  be  able  to  be  accessed  by  a  user  with  
sufficient
 
permissions
 2.2.  The  system  shall  protect  AI  request  and  AI  response  data  from  
unauthorized
 
access.
 2.3.  The  system  shall  protect  sensitive  data  to  be  modified  by  robots.    
3.  Reliability  3.1.  The  system  shall  retain  operational  logs  for  at  least  12  months.  3.2.  The  system  shall  have  daily  backups  3.3.  The  system  shall  continue  operating  if  there  is  an  issue  
 4.  Maintainability  and  Coding  Standards  4.1.  The  system  shall  host  AI  queries  and  structural  queries  separately  4.2.  The  database  schema  shall  be  well  documented.  4.3.  SQL  keywords  shall  be  written  in  upper  case  
 
5.  Scalability  5.1.  The  system  shall  support  growth  in  the  number  of  robots  and  deployment  
locations.
 5.2.  The  system  shall  support  increasing  volumes  of  task  execution  and  logs  
data.
 5.3.   The  system  shall  support  any  future  AI  integration.

6.  Privacy  6.1.  Access  to  emotional  interaction  records  shall  be  restricted.  6.2.  Access  to  emotional  data  shall  be  restricted  to  authorized  persons  only.  6.3.  Access  to  operator  information  from  the  home  subclass  shall  be  restricted.   7.  Storage  7.1.  Emotional  interaction  records  shall  be  stored  securely  to  protect  human-  
related
 
sensitive
 
data.
 7.2.  The  system  shall  store  logs,  task  history,  charging  session  history.  7.3.  The  system  shall  store  high  volumes  of  data  related  to  performance  
metrics
 
and
 
AI
 
responses.

Project  Description  
 
This  project  focuses  on  designing  a  database  system  to  manage  fleets  of  
humanoid
 
robots
 
used
 
in
 
environments
 
such
 
as
 
warehouses,
 
hospitals,
 
and
 
homes.
 
As
 
humanoid
 
robots
 
become
 
more
 
common,
 
they
 
generate
 
a
 
large
 
amount
 
of
 
information,
 
including
 
task
 
assignments,
 
task
 
execution
 
history,
 
deployment
 
locations,
 
energy
 
levels,
 
maintenance
 
records,
 
and
 
interaction
 
details.
 
Currently,
 
much
 
of
 
this
 
information
 
is
 
either
 
spread
 
across
 
different
 
tools
 
or
 
not
 
stored
 
in
 
a
 
structured
 
way,
 
making
 
it
 
difficult
 
to
 
track
 
robot
 
behavior
 
and
 
performance
 
over
 
time.
 
There  are  existing  robot  fleet  management  platforms,  such  as  Formant  and  
InOrbit
,
 
that
 
help
 
monitor
 
robots
 
and
 
show
 
their
 
current
 
status.
 
These
 
systems
 
are
 
useful
 
for
 
real-time
 
monitoring
 
and
 
coordination,
 
but
 
they
 
are
 
mainly
 
designed
 
for
 
general
 
robot
 
fleets
 
and
 
do
 
not
 
focus
 
on
 
organizing
 
long-term
 
data
 
for
 
humanoid
 
robots.
 
In
 
particular,
 
they
 
do
 
not
 
emphasize
 
storing
 
detailed
 
interaction
 
context,
 
coordination
 
roles,
 
or
 
historical
 
data
 
in
 
a
 
centralized
 
database.
 
One  unique  feature  of  the  system  is  its  ability  to  store  human  emotional  
context
 
data
 
in
 
personal
 
and
 
healthcare
 
environments.
 
In
 
settings
 
such
 
as
 
hospitals
 
or
 
homes,
 
humanoid
 
robots
 
often
 
interact
 
closely
 
with
 
people.
 
The
 
system
 
allows
 
simple
 
emotional
 
observations,
 
such
 
as
 
calm
 
or
 
urgent
 
situations,
 
to
 
be
 
recorded
 
as
 
data
 
for
 
later
 
analysis.
 
Most
 
existing
 
fleet
 
management
 
tools
 
do
 
not
 
capture
 
or
 
organize
 
this
 
type
 
of
 
information.
 
Another  unique  feature  is  role-based  robot  coordination  in  warehouse  
environments.
 
The
 
system
 
allows
 
certain
 
robots
 
to
 
be
 
assigned
 
a
 
senior
 
role
 
to
 
help
 
coordinate
 
tasks
 
among
 
other
 
robots
 
based
 
on
 
factors
 
such
 
as
 
hardware
